name: Mobile App Development
description: Proposal template for native or cross-platform mobile application development. Covers UX design, development, testing, and App Store deployment.
author: ScopeForge

workstreams.name: QA, device testing & App Store submission

details.title: Mobile App Development Proposal
details.subtitle: Cross-platform mobile experience — from design to App Store launch
details.recommendation: Begin with a focused MVP targeting your core mobile use case, then iterate based on user feedback.
executiveSummary: A well-crafted mobile app can significantly increase customer engagement and retention.
We recommend a cross-platform approach (React Native or Flutter) to maximize reach while controlling cost.
The MVP focuses on your highest-value mobile use case, enabling fast launch and validated learning.
whatWeHeard: Your customers increasingly expect a mobile-first experience.
The current web experience doesn't translate well to mobile devices.
Speed to market is important — you need to validate demand before investing in platform-wide features.
details.investmentSummary: Total investment ranges from $120K for an MVP launch to $200K for a full-featured release, with milestone-based payments tied to deliverables.

valueProposal.headline: Meet your customers where they are — on mobile
valueProposal.narrative: A purpose-built mobile app increases engagement, enables self-service, and opens new revenue channels that aren't possible on web alone.
unlocks: Higher customer engagement — mobile users interact 3x more than web users
Self-service capability — let customers manage their experience from anywhere
Push notification channel — re-engage users with timely, relevant updates

valueSources.label: Mobile customer engagement lift
valueSources.currentState: Web-only experience with limited mobile responsiveness
valueSources.futureState: Native mobile experience with optimized workflows

valueSources.label: Customer self-service via mobile
valueSources.currentState: Customers contact support for routine tasks
valueSources.futureState: In-app self-service for account management and support

buildPlan.name: Product discovery & UX
buildPlan.description: Define user journeys, create wireframes, and establish the design system for the mobile app.

buildPlan.name: MVP development
buildPlan.description: Build the core mobile experience with key features for launch.

buildPlan.name: QA & App Store submission
buildPlan.description: Comprehensive testing across devices, performance optimization, and App Store submission.

buildPlan.name: Post-launch support & iteration
buildPlan.description: Monitor launch metrics, fix issues, and plan the next iteration based on user feedback.

actualDeliverables.title: Mobile app (iOS + Android)
actualDeliverables.description: Cross-platform mobile application with native look and feel on both platforms.

actualDeliverables.title: Backend API
actualDeliverables.description: RESTful or GraphQL API powering the mobile app.
acceptanceCriteria: Load test passes for expected traffic

actualDeliverables.title: Design system & assets
actualDeliverables.description: Complete design system with components, icons, and guidelines.
acceptanceCriteria: Design system covers all app screens

actualDeliverables.title: App Store listing
actualDeliverables.description: Prepare and submit the app to both Apple App Store and Google Play.

pricing.summary: Milestone-based pricing tied to deliverables. MVP at $120K; full release at $200K.

terms.paymentTerms: 30% upon signing, 30% at MVP milestone, 20% at QA completion, 20% at App Store launch.
startConditions: Design system or brand guidelines if available
assumptions: Cross-platform framework (React Native or Flutter) is suitable for the target use case.
Client provides App Store and Google Play developer accounts.
Third-party API integrations are available and documented.
exclusions: Ongoing hosting, CI/CD, or infrastructure costs beyond initial setup.
Wearable (Apple Watch, Wear OS) or tablet-specific layouts.
clientResponsibilities: Provide product requirements and acceptance criteria.
Review designs and builds at each milestone.
Provide App Store developer accounts and certificates.
terms.changeControl: Feature changes after the discovery phase are handled via written change orders with updated estimates.
terms.expiration: This proposal is valid for 30 days from the date of issue.

footer.confidentiality: This proposal is confidential and prepared exclusively for {{COMPANY_NAME}}.
footer.contact: Questions? Reach out to your ScopeForge account team.

nextSteps: Schedule a scoping call to align on MVP features.
Share any existing design assets or brand guidelines.
Confirm App Store developer account access.
